item.title: Skin-Safe 3D Printing Materials: ISO 10993 Biocompatibility for Beauty Devices
item.description: Not all 3D printing resins are safe for sustained skin contact. This guide covers which materials pass ISO 10993 biocompatibility testing and what that means for facial device development.

item.title: About 3D Print Skincare
item.description:
Where Beauty Meets Advanced Manufacturing

3D Print Skincare covers the intersection of additive manufacturing and personal care — the niche where a beauty brand’s CAD file becomes a production-ready facial device without six months of mold tooling, and where a formulator’s lab needs a custom patch-testing fixture that no catalog supplier stocks.

The site is published by Ben Stagl and Max Davis (ChiLab Studio, Chicago), with manufacturing expertise spanning 20 years of fabrication, SAIC professorship in designed objects, and CNC/digital fabrication at MAXCNC. Our collaborators include formulation chemists, product design studios, and contract manufacturers across the beauty supply chain.

item.title: Advertise on 3D Print Skincare
item.description:
Advertise on 3D Print Skincare

Our audience includes beauty brand founders, product development managers, formulation chemists, and industrial designers sourcing manufacturing for skincare devices, packaging, and lab components. This is a high-intent, premium audience.

Audience Profile

  • Job titles: Product development director, formulation scientist, brand founder, packaging designer
  • Industry: Beauty, personal care, medical aesthetics, contract manufacturing
  • Intent: Actively evaluating 3D printing for device prototyping or short-run production

Advertising Packages

Package Rate Placement
Banner Ad (728×90) $500/month Homepage leaderboard
Sponsored Content $1,200/month Featured service article
Network Package $3,500/month All PrintWire sites

Ideal partners: SLA/MJF service bureaus, biocompatible material suppliers, beauty packaging consultants, skincare device contract manufacturers.

item.title: Services
item.description:
Facial Tool Manufacturing

Additive manufacturing removes the tooling barrier for beauty brands developing custom facial tools. Gua sha stones (printed in ceramic-composite or stone-filled resin), facial rollers with precision bearing housings, cleansing brush heads with defined bristle-hole geometry, and microneedling device handles can all be produced in production quantities of 1–5,000 units without mold investment. Skin-contact materials: medical-grade SLA resins (ISO 10993 biocompatibility), polypropylene (SLS), and TPU for flexible applicator heads.
